Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and complete main.py to implement all Flask routes for the 'SmartHomeManager' web application. Ensure that each route correctly loads and processes data from the local text files in the 'data' directory as specified in the requirements, and renders the corresponding HTML templates. The routes to implement and fix include  dashboard (root '/'), device_list, device_control (with device_id parameter), add_device (supporting GET and POST), automation_rules, energy_report, and activity_logs. The main.py must handle data parsing, business logic, and pass all necessary data to templates for rendering. Also, ensure the 'data' directory is created if missing.
Composition.Programmer 2: Create all required HTML template files for the 'SmartHomeManager' web application inside the Flask 'templates' directory. Templates to create are  dashboard.html, device_list.html, device_control.html, add_device.html, automation_rules.html, energy_report.html, and activity_logs.html. Each template must include the specified page title and all required elements with the exact IDs as defined in the requirements document. The templates should be structured to receive and display data passed from main.py, including dynamic content such as device lists, automation rules, energy data, and activity logs. Ensure templates support navigation buttons linking to the correct routes.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of the following elements on each page

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
